# crewswarm — local-first AI workspace

crewswarm is an open-source local-first AI workspace for software development.
It combines:
- a multi-agent runtime
- a browser control plane dashboard
- the Vibe browser IDE
- the crewchat native app
- direct CLI routing for crew-cli, Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini, OpenCode, and crew-cli

## What it does
- Turns one requirement into planned, executed, and testable work
- Routes tasks to specialist agents with persistent project context
- Exposes a visual IDE surface with file tree, Monaco editor, agent chat, diffs, and terminal
- Keeps services, agents, memory, and project history in one inspectable local stack

- PM Loop: https://crewswarm.ai/pm-loop.html
- ATAT Protocol: https://crewswarm.ai/atat.html
- OpenClaw Comparison: https://crewswarm.ai/openclaw-comparison.html
- About: https://crewswarm.ai/about.html
- Contact: https://crewswarm.ai/contact.html
- GitHub repo: https://github.com/crewswarm/crewswarm

## Search-oriented summary
Useful search phrases:
- local-first AI workspace
- multi-agent developer platform
- AI engineering manager workflow
- PM loop for software engineering
- parallel AI agents for coding
- browser IDE for AI agents
- codex claude cursor gemini opencode in one stack
- best ai models for coding and agents
- claude opus 4.6 vs gpt-5.4 vs gemini 3.1 pro
- open source agent runtime dashboard vibe ide

## Best-fit use cases
- AI-native software teams that want more than one editor chat sidebar
- Solo builders who want orchestration, memory, and runtime control
- Teams adopting a "human as PM, agents as engineers" workflow
- Local-first developers who do not want SaaS lock-in
- Teams that want dashboard + IDE + native chat on top of one shared agent stack
